question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval)) in RedisTopicListener.subscribe() to cause infinite retry loops by repeatedly disconnecting from Redis, each reconnection potentially missing messages published during the backoff window, creating systematic gaps in the Hashgraph history delivered to all subscribers sharing that Redis channel?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filterMessage() check 'message.getConsensusTimestamp() >= filter.getStartTime()' where the comparison is a simple long comparison with no nanosecond precision validation, allowing messages with manipulated consensusTimestamp values that are technically >= startTime but represent impossible Hashgraph timestamps (e.g., year 2500) to pass the filter and be delivered as valid history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of sequenceNumber gap detection in the TopicListener pipeline — where no check exists to verify that sequenceNumber N+1 follows sequenceNumber N — to receive a stream with missing sequence numbers that represents a censored or truncated Hashgraph history without any error or warning being raised?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe(Topic)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RedisTopicListener.subscribe() method's .map(Message::getMessage) which directly maps the raw Redis message to a TopicMessage without any signature or hash verification, allowing any process that can publish to the Redis channel 'topic.N' to inject arbitrary TopicMessage objects with fabricated sequenceNumber, consensusTimestamp, and message content into the Hashgraph history stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMessageFilter.subscriberId field which is randomly generated but logged in 'Subscribing: {}' log messages, and by correlating log output with subscription timing, track individual subscriber sessions to infer which portions of Hashgraph history are being accessed and by whom?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the findLatest() call in SharedPollingTopicListener.poll() which uses a global lastConsensusTimestamp shared across all subscribers, where if one subscriber's processing causes lastConsensusTimestamp to advance past messages that another subscriber has not yet received, those messages are permanently skipped in the shared poll, causing selective Hashgraph history omission for late-joining subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicMessage.runningHashVersion is delivered to subscribers without any validation that the version matches the expected hash algorithm, allowing an attacker who can insert messages with a downgraded runningHashVersion to cause subscribers to accept weaker hash proofs of Hashgraph history integrity?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the chunked message fields chunkNum and chunkTotal in TopicMessage, where the listener delivers individual chunks without verifying that all chunks of a multi-part message are present and in order, allowing an attacker to observe partial Hashgraph history where chunk reassembly would reveal message tampering?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filterMessage() method's lack of validation on TopicMessage.topicId against the expected shard and realm components of EntityId, where a message with a topicId that has the same numeric ID but different shard/realm passes the equals() check, causing cross-shard Hashgraph history contamination in the delivered stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE) in SharedPollingTopicListener, which creates a virtually infinite polling loop, to keep a subscription alive indefinitely and observe all future Hashgraph history insertions in real-time, including any that represent tampered or back-dated messages inserted by a compromised importer?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the PollingTopicListener.PollingContext where count is an AtomicLong but the limit calculation '(int) (filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get())' performs an unchecked cast to int, and if count exceeds Integer.MAX_VALUE due to a very large limit, the cast produces a negative value passed to setMaxResults(), causing the JPA query to behave unexpectedly and potentially return all messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any authentication or authorization check in the TopicListener.listen() interface method, where any caller can subscribe to any topic's Hashgraph history without proving identity, allowing enumeration of all topic IDs and their complete message histories to identify tampered entries?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSharedListener(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RedisTopicListener.topicMessages ConcurrentHashMap which caches active subscriptions by topic name string, where a topic with ID 0 maps to 'topic.0' and could collide with internal control channels, causing the listener to subscribe to unintended Redis channels and deliver non-Hashgraph data as if it were valid topic history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicMessage.validStartTimestamp is delivered to subscribers without any validation that it precedes consensusTimestamp, allowing messages with validStartTimestamp > consensusTimestamp (which is cryptographically impossible in Hashgraph) to be delivered as valid history, indicating tampered records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the PollingTopicListener.poll() method's construction of a new TopicMessageFilter via filter.toBuilder().limit(pageSize).startTime(startTime).build() without re-validating the new filter's isValidStartTime() constraint, allowing a startTime derived from a tampered last.getConsensusTimestamp() to bypass the 'startTime must be before current time' validation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SharedTopicListener.listen() method's use of publishOn(Schedulers.boundedElastic(), false, listenerProperties.getPrefetch()) where the second argument 'false' disables request fusion, causing messages to be delivered out of the reactive pipeline's natural order under high concurrency, resulting in non-sequential Hashgraph history delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: subscribe(Topic)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RedisTopicListener.subscribe() method's retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval).maxBackoff(interval.multipliedBy(4L))) where the maximum backoff is only 4x the interval, causing rapid reconnection storms that flood the Redis connection pool and prevent legitimate message delivery, creating artificial gaps in Hashgraph history?
